case.title: SOUTHERN PACIFIC v. PUBLIC UTILITIES COMM., CALIFORNIA

opinion.h4: SOUTHERN PACIFIC v. PUBLIC UTILITIES COMM., CALIFORNIA, (1969)
SOUTHERN PACIFIC CO. v. PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION OF CALIFORNIA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF CALIFORNIA. No. 1050. Decided May 5, 1969.
opinion.p: The motion to dismiss is granted and the appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
MR. JUSTICE BLACK and MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS are of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
opinion.h4: SOUTHERN PACIFIC v. PUBLIC UTILITIES COMM., CALIFORNIA, (1969)
opinion.p: SOUTHERN PACIFIC CO. v. PUBLIC UTILITIES COMMISSION OF CALIFORNIA ET AL. APPEAL FROM THE UNITED STATES DISTRICT COURT FOR THE NORTHERN DISTRICT OF CALIFORNIA. No. 1152. Decided May 5, 1969.
Mary Moran Pajalich for appellee Public Utilities Commission of California.
The motion to affirm is granted and the judgment is affirmed.
MR. JUSTICE BLACK and MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS are of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
